##NAMES=Howlite
##RRUFFID=R110150
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_SiB_5_O_9_(OH)_5_
##LOCALITY=Potash Corporation of Saskatchewan mine, Penobsquis, Kings County, New Brunswick, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Donald M Doell and Brian Roulston
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless bladed crystals, extracted by dissolving the matrix of sylvite/halite, associated with strontioginorite R110149, volkovskite R110151, ruitenbergite R110152, congolite R110153, and hilgardite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R110150
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.611 b: 9.361 c: 12.822 alpha: 90 beta: 104.791 gamma: 90 volume: 999.3 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
